超.mnemonic: Running past a calling shout — surpassing everything.

趣.mnemonic: Running toward the ear's pleasure — pursuing one's interest.

距.mnemonic: A foot keeping its distance from a giant — measuring space apart.

跡.mnemonic: Foot traces left behind in red earth — a mark or vestige.

路.mnemonic: Each foot step on a journey traces out a road.

践.mnemonic: A foot steps forward in war — treading the ground.

踊.mnemonic: The foot leaps through a passage — dancing freely.

踏.mnemonic: A foot presses down on water and rice — stomping firmly.

財.mnemonic: A scholar's talent turns into financial wealth.

貢.mnemonic: Work the land to pay tribute in shells to the ruler.

貧.mnemonic: Divide your money shells and become poor.

販.mnemonic: A returning merchant sells goods for shells.

貫.mnemonic: Thread shells onto a string to string coins together.

責.mnemonic: Bundle thorns with shells — you are held responsible for the debt.

貯.mnemonic: Store your shells in a warehouse to save money.

貰.mnemonic: Someone takes shells from a rack — they receive them as a gift.

貴.mnemonic: Middle and shells together make something precious and noble.

貸.mnemonic: Substitute shells for another — lend money in place of them.

貼.mnemonic: Use a shell-coin to stick and paste things flat.

貿.mnemonic: Cut through shells with trade — exchange goods across borders.

賑.mnemonic: Shake shells around and the market becomes lively and bustling.

賜.mnemonic: An easy gift of shells bestowed from above by the ruler.

賞.mnemonic: A prize of shells is given to the top warrior — a reward.

身.mnemonic: A person's rounded belly — the body itself.

軍.mnemonic: Wagons covered under a roof — an army on the move.

軟.mnemonic: A vehicle made of soft wood bends rather than breaks.

軽.mnemonic: A vehicle that goes straight and fast feels light.

載.mnemonic: A halberd loads cargo onto a vehicle — to carry/record.

輝.mnemonic: A wheel of light from the sun — to shine and radiate.

輩.mnemonic: Many wheels lined up in a row — a generation of fellows.

輪.mnemonic: A wheel made from gathered wood forms a ring.

輸.mnemonic: Organized wagons transport goods — to export/convey.

辛.mnemonic: A sharp stake driven in — bitterness and hardship.

辟.mnemonic: A crouching person by a bitter stake faces punishment.

辱.mnemonic: A hand under a biting stake presses down in shame.

農.mnemonic: A bent figure working the land at dawn — a farmer.

辶.mnemonic: A foot stepping forward on a road — the movement radical.

辺.mnemonic: A knife-like shape moving along a road — a nearby edge.

辿.mnemonic: Follow the mountain path step by step.

迄.mnemonic: Until the extreme end of the road.

迅.mnemonic: Move swiftly like a bird along the road.

近.mnemonic: An axe is nearby — close enough to chop.

迫.mnemonic: A white cliff pressing close, leaving no room to move.

述.mnemonic: Walk through ideas to narrate a story along the way.

迷.mnemonic: A rice field in every direction — totally lost on the road.

退.mnemonic: Walk away and stop — to retreat from the road.

透.mnemonic: Walk through a path so clear it becomes transparent.

逞.mnemonic: Move boldly along the road, showing strength and power.

速.mnemonic: Bundle up and dash down the road — fast!

造.mnemonic: Tell a story on the road to create something new.

逮.mnemonic: Chase someone down the road to catch them.

遇.mnemonic: Walk a corner of the road and unexpectedly meet someone.

運.mnemonic: An army moves along the road — that's fate and fortune.

道.mnemonic: A person's head leads the way down the road.

遜.mnemonic: A grandchild humbly yields and steps aside.

遡.mnemonic: Moving against the flow, tracing back upstream.

遣.mnemonic: A skilled hand dispatches a messenger down the road.

適.mnemonic: Moving along the road to a fitting destination.

邑.mnemonic: A boundary enclosing people — a settlement or town.

邪.mnemonic: A settlement gone crooked becomes evil and wicked.

郊.mnemonic: Mixing crops near the village marks the suburbs.

郵.mnemonic: Hanging mail is delivered to the next village post.

都.mnemonic: Many people gather at the village to form a capital city.

酉.mnemonic: A sake jar with a lid — the vessel of wine and the rooster hour.

酔.mnemonic: Nine rounds of sake from the jar and you're drunk.

酬.mnemonic: A toast from the sake jar repays a kind gesture.

酷.mnemonic: The sake jar tells a harsh, severe truth.

酸.mnemonic: The sake jar turns sour under the sun with scattered drops.

闇.mnemonic: A door closed in darkness hides the sound inside — pure dark.

闌.mnemonic: East meets west at the gate — a barrier at its peak.

闘.mnemonic: Two fighters clash at the gate — a fierce battle.

阜.mnemonic: A mound rising high — a symbol of an earthen hill.

阝.mnemonic: A slender mound or village sign — context tells left from right.

防.mnemonic: A mound on the left blocks the way — a defense embankment.

阻.mnemonic: A mound blocks your path like a blockade — obstruction.

院.mnemonic: A complete walled mound enclosing a courtyard — an institution.

陰.mnemonic: A mound now covered — shade and shadow creep in.

陶.mnemonic: A mound of clay shaped by hand into fine pottery.

陸.mnemonic: A mound of solid earth piled high — dry land.

陽.mnemonic: The sun rises over the mound, flooding it with light — the sunny side.

隅.mnemonic: A mound tucked into a corner — a remote nook.

隊.mnemonic: People follow the mound-trail in a line — a troop formation.

階.mnemonic: Mounds stacked like steps lead you all — a stairway of levels.

野.mnemonic: Rice fields stretch across the village — wild open country.

鈍.mnemonic: A metal blade that moves slowly — dull and blunt.

鉄.mnemonic: Metal that loses its arrow-like shine — iron.

鉛.mnemonic: Metal shaped like a crawling creature — soft lead.

鉢.mnemonic: Metal divided at the base — a bowl or pot.

銀.mnemonic: Metal that glints like a sharp eye — silver.

銃.mnemonic: Metal shaped like a fused pipe — a gun.

銭.mnemonic: Two spears of metal cut through commerce — coins/money.

錠.mnemonic: A metal piece set firmly in place — a lock or tablet.

錯.mnemonic: Metal ideas criss-cross in the past — confusion/mix.

鍛.mnemonic: Metal struck with a forge hammer repeatedly — to temper/forge.

鍵.mnemonic: Metal shaped like a fist on a string — a key.

鏡.mnemonic: Metal surface that competes with your own image — a mirror.

鐘.mnemonic: Metal hung heavy like a child's toy — a temple bell.

鑑.mnemonic: Metal watched over by a careful eye — a mirror/model.

隔.mnemonic: A mound separates each compartment, keeping things apart.

隙.mnemonic: A small mound beside a crack where sunlight seeps through a gap.

際.mnemonic: A mound beside a sacrificial altar marks the border between lands.

障.mnemonic: A mound rising with a badge blocks the way — an obstacle.

隠.mnemonic: Behind a mound, a hand hides something — concealed.

隣.mnemonic: A mound next to rice and a child — the neighbor lives close.

隶.mnemonic: A hand reaching down to grab — depicts a captive or slave.

隹.mnemonic: A bird with a short tail perched — the short-tailed bird radical.

雇.mnemonic: A door beneath a short-tailed bird — hire a bird to work.

離.mnemonic: A bird entangled in a net then separates — to leave and part.

雪.mnemonic: Rain falls and a hand sweeps it aside — that is snow.

雰.mnemonic: Rain above a divided share — misty atmosphere fills the air.

雲.mnemonic: Rain above a swirling shape — clouds carry the rain.

雷.mnemonic: Rain above a rice field crackling — thunder strikes the paddy.

需.mnemonic: Rain falls on a man who waits — he needs it, a necessity.

魚.mnemonic: A fish head on top, body in the middle, and tail fins below — a whole fish.

鮮.mnemonic: Fresh fish caught by sheep herders — the freshest catch on the mountain.

鳩.mnemonic: Nine birds perched together — a flock of doves cooing.

鹿.mnemonic: A proud deer standing tall with antlers spread wide.

麗.mnemonic: Deer with beautiful twin crowns of flowers — a gorgeous, elegant sight.

黄.mnemonic: Fields of wheat glowing golden under the sun — yellow abundance.

龍.mnemonic: A mighty dragon standing tall, scales rippling, soaring through the sky.

飽.mnemonic: Food plus wrapping means you've eaten enough to be satisfied.

餃.mnemonic: Food folded like crossed hands — that's a dumpling.

館.mnemonic: Food and official building make a grand hall or inn.

首.mnemonic: Hair over eyes — the neck and head together form a person's face.

香.mnemonic: Grain ripening in the sun gives off a sweet fragrance.

馬.mnemonic: A pictograph of a horse with mane, body, and four legs — just a horse.

駅.mnemonic: A horse stopping at a post station for a relay — a train station.

駆.mnemonic: A horse spurred forward by a master — to drive or gallop.

騒.mnemonic: Horses and fleas stirring up a frantic noisy commotion.

驚.mnemonic: A respectful bow before a horse startles it — to be surprised.

骨.mnemonic: Flesh over bone — the joint and flesh together make a skeleton.

髪.mnemonic: Long hair flowing down, shaped by the hand — beautiful hair.

鬱.mnemonic: Trees, container, and fragrance all trapped — deeply gloomy depression.

鬲.mnemonic: A pictograph of an ancient three-legged cooking cauldron — a tripod pot.

魅.mnemonic: A demon with a ghost spirit casts a bewitching charm over you.

震.mnemonic: Rain falls as a dragon shakes the earth — thunder trembles below.

霊.mnemonic: Rain over a shaman's ritual fire — a spirit descends.

霧.mnemonic: Rain over dense growth creates a misty fog.

静.mnemonic: Azure color of a dispute settled quietly — stillness.

革.mnemonic: A hide stretched and tanned flat — leather reformed.

靴.mnemonic: Leather shaped into a flower-like form — a shoe.

鞄.mnemonic: Leather wrapped around a package — a bag or briefcase.

韋.mnemonic: A hide tanned on both sides — soft leather, the base radical.

頂.mnemonic: A nail-point atop a head marks the very summit.

頃.mnemonic: A small head bowed in time — about this moment.

順.mnemonic: A river flowing past a head goes along smoothly — in order.

預.mnemonic: A warning head-nod in advance — deposit it ahead of time.

類.mnemonic: Rice and a dog's head sorted together — a category or type.

顧.mnemonic: An old bird turns its head to look back — to look after.

飼.mnemonic: Food given on command — to raise and feed animals.
